title: Well contamination after floodwater contact
summary: A stop-use, alternative-water, inspection, treatment, and testing sequence for a private well that may have been submerged or contaminated by floodwater.
content:
A stop-use, alternative-water, inspection, treatment, and testing sequence for a private well that may have been submerged or contaminated by floodwater.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Stop drinking and food use immediately when floodwater reached the wellhead, pump, wiring, or surrounding ground.
Use bottled or otherwise officially verified water for people, infants, pets, medicines, ice, and tooth brushing.
Keep away from damaged electrical equipment and arrange professional inspection, cleaning, disinfection, and repair after floodwater recedes.
Test through the competent authority or accredited service and resume use only after acceptable results and any required flushing or repeat testing.
warnings: Clear-looking well water can still contain microbes or chemicals.
Do not energize a flooded pump or enter a well chamber.
common_mistakes: Starting Well contamination after floodwater contact without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Well contamination after floodwater contact.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Well contamination after floodwater contact when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Well contamination after floodwater contact when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Well contamination after floodwater contact.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Well contamination after floodwater contact safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Well contamination after floodwater contact when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Well contamination after floodwater contact.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Well contamination after floodwater contact should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Well contamination after floodwater contact whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Well contamination after floodwater contact in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Well contamination after floodwater contact after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Well contamination after floodwater contact cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Well contamination after floodwater contact are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Well contamination after floodwater contact to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Well contamination after floodwater contact.']
